mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals continued labor income generation, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This is an always-on momentum channel independent of regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month trend and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive PAYEMS momentum periods show no statistically significant excess return versus unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts exceeding a threshold signals contraction in residential investment that historically precedes broader demand weakness and credit stress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as the housing downturn propagates to consumer and financial sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts have been declining steadily through 2006-2007 amid subprime mortgage stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where HOUST YoY decline exceeds 15%, or the effect disappears when tested pre-2000 and post-2010 samples separately.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of the federal funds rate and CPI inflation determines the real policy rate; when FEDFUNDS declines while CPIAUCSL growth remains stable, the resulting drop in real rates lowers equity discount rates and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. Neither series alone captures this effect; the interaction of nominal rate change and inflation level is required.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the real-rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY) against forward 20d SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or single-series FEDFUNDS model matches or exceeds the interaction model's predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime with historically low unemployment, any upward inflection in UNRATE signals labor market turning point and impending slowdown, which markets have not yet priced, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as the cycle turns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.0% is near cycle lows as of mid-2007
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with unemployment near multi-year lows
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns following a UNRATE uptick of 0.1pp or more in a late-cycle low-unemployment regime are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or the effect is not distinguishable from random walk in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Short-horizon acceleration in industrial production reflects improving supply-side output and manufacturing momentum that is quickly priced into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week. This is a persistent trend-following effect active regardless of regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and forward 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in retail sales growth below trend signals weakening consumer demand, which is a leading indicator of slowing corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month once the deceleration crosses a meaningful threshold.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns following a RSAFS growth deceleration below the 2% threshold are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of acute credit market stress, a rapid downward move in the federal funds rate or explicit signaling of imminent cuts triggers a short-horizon relief rally in equities as markets price in liquidity support, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS effective rate or futures-implied path drops more than 25bp within a 2-week window during a credit stress episode
evidence_refs: fact:August 2007 credit market disruption following subprime mortgage stress prompted expectations of Fed intervention
regime_hint: acute credit market stress / financial crisis onset
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following a rapid FEDFUNDS easing signal during stress episodes are negative or insignificant (p > 0.05) in more than half of historical stress episodes tested.

mechanism_narrative: The joint deterioration of housing starts and retail sales signals a broad-based demand contraction spanning both durable investment and consumption, a combination with stronger recessionary signal content than either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts have declined steadily through 2006-2007 while retail sales growth has moderated
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-RSAFS decline signal fails to outperform either single-series model in out-of-sample Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or SPX forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of jointly-triggered instances.
